title_en: Author Biography and History of Islamic Jurisprudence
content_en:
Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Hajwi ath-Tha'alibi al-Fasi was a Moroccan Maliki scholar born in Fez in 1291 AH (1874 CE). He came from a distinguished scholarly family of Moroccan origin and received a thorough traditional education at the Qarawiyyin mosque-university — one of the oldest universities in the world — before becoming one of the leading Islamic legal scholars of early twentieth-century Morocco.

Al-Hajwi served in a number of important positions in the Moroccan religious-legal establishment, including as a teacher at the Qarawiyyin and as a senior official in the Moroccan government during both the pre-Protectorate and Protectorate periods. His engagement with French colonial rule of Morocco was complex: he sought to preserve Islamic scholarly institutions while also advocating for the engagement of Islamic scholarship with the challenges of modernity.

His major historical work, Al-Fikr as-Sami fi Tarikh al-Fiqh al-Islami (The Elevated Thought in the History of Islamic Jurisprudence), is a comprehensive history of Islamic legal thought from its origins in the Quran and Sunnah through its development in the four major legal schools and their subsequent histories up to the author's own time. The work runs to four volumes in modern editions.

Al-Fikr as-Sami occupies a distinctive position in Islamic intellectual history as one of the few classical-style scholarly works to address the historical development of fiqh as a historical question rather than simply as a tradition to be transmitted. Al-Hajwi examined how Islamic law developed, why the various legal schools emerged when and where they did, and how the fiqh tradition had changed across centuries — questions that required both deep legal knowledge and historical perspective.

He died in Rabat in 1376 AH (1956 CE), shortly after Moroccan independence, having witnessed both the threat to traditional Islamic scholarship posed by colonial rule and the beginnings of its institutional recovery.
title_id: Biografi Pengarang dan Sejarah Fikih Islam
content_id:
Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Hajwi ats-Tsa'alibi al-Fasi adalah ulama Maliki Maroko yang lahir di Fez pada tahun 1291 H (1874 M). Ia berasal dari keluarga ulama terkemuka berdarah Maroko dan menerima pendidikan tradisional yang menyeluruh di masjid-universitas Qarawiyyin — salah satu universitas tertua di dunia — sebelum menjadi salah satu ulama hukum Islam terkemuka di Maroko pada awal abad kedua puluh.

Al-Hajwi memegang sejumlah posisi penting dalam lembaga keagamaan-hukum Maroko, termasuk sebagai pengajar di Qarawiyyin dan sebagai pejabat senior dalam pemerintahan Maroko selama periode sebelum Protektorat maupun Protektorat. Keterlibatannya dengan pemerintahan kolonial Prancis di Maroko bersifat kompleks: ia berusaha melestarikan lembaga-lembaga keilmuan Islam sekaligus menganjurkan keterlibatan keilmuan Islam dengan tantangan-tantangan modernitas.

Karya historis utamanya, Al-Fikr as-Sami fi Tarikh al-Fiqh al-Islami (Pemikiran Agung dalam Sejarah Fikih Islam), adalah sejarah komprehensif pemikiran hukum Islam dari asal-usulnya dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah melalui perkembangannya dalam empat mazhab hukum utama dan sejarah-sejarah mereka berikutnya hingga zaman pengarang sendiri. Karya ini mencapai empat jilid dalam cetakan modern.

Al-Fikr as-Sami menempati posisi khas dalam sejarah intelektual Islam sebagai salah satu dari sedikit karya ilmiah bergaya klasik yang membahas perkembangan historis fikih sebagai pertanyaan historis daripada sekadar sebagai tradisi yang harus ditransmisikan. Al-Hajwi memeriksa bagaimana hukum Islam berkembang, mengapa berbagai mazhab hukum muncul kapan dan di mana mereka muncul, dan bagaimana tradisi fikih telah berubah lintas abad — pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang memerlukan pengetahuan hukum yang mendalam sekaligus perspektif historis.

Ia wafat di Rabat pada tahun 1376 H (1956 M), tak lama setelah kemerdekaan Maroko, setelah menyaksikan baik ancaman terhadap keilmuan Islam tradisional yang ditimbulkan oleh pemerintahan kolonial maupun awal-awal pemulihan institusionalnya.
content_ar: محمد ﷺ ibn al-حسن al-Hajwi ath-Tha'alibi al-Fasi was a Moroccan المالكي scholar born in Fez in 1291 AH (1874 CE). He came from a distinguished العلمي الأسرة of Moroccan origin and received a thorough التقليدي education at the Qarawiyyin المسجد-university — one of the oldest universities in the الدنيا — قبل becoming one of the leading Islamic legal العلماء of early twentieth-century Morocco. Al-Hajwi served in a number of المهم positions in the Moroccan religious-legal establishment, بما في ذلك as a teacher at the Qarawiyyin and as a senior official in the Moroccan government خلال both the pre-Protectorate and Protectorate periods. His engagement with French colonial rule of Morocco was complex: he sought to preserve Islamic العلمي institutions بينما أيضاً advocating for the engagement of Islamic scholarship with the challenges of modernity. His major historical work, Al-Fikr as-Sami fi Tarikh al-الفقه al-Islami (The Elevated Thought in the History of Islamic Jurisprudence), is a comprehensive history of Islamic legal thought from its origins in the القرآن الكريم and السنة through its development in the four major legal schools and their subsequent histories up to the author's own time. The work runs to four volumes in modern editions. Al-Fikr as-Sami occupies a distinctive position in Islamic intellectual history as one of the few الكلاسيكي-style العلمي works to address the historical development of الفقه as a historical question rather than simply as a التراث to be transmitted. Al-Hajwi examined how Islamic law developed, why the various legal schools emerged عندما and where they did, and how the الفقه التراث had changed across centuries — questions that required both deep legal العلم and historical perspective. He died in Rabat in 1376 AH (1956 CE), shortly بعد Moroccan independence, having witnessed both the threat to التقليدي Islamic scholarship posed by colonial rule and the beginnings of its institutional recovery.
